findings.title: AdminTopbar notification + user dropdowns lack z-[60] override (BUG-002 follow-up)
findings.evidence: DropdownMenuContent classes do NOT include z-[60] like the fixed DashboardTopbar:89. Lines 269 and 322 use class="w-80 ... p-0" and class="w-56 ..." with no z-index override. Primitive dropdown-menu-content.svelte:26 has baked-in z-50. Header at AdminTopbar.svelte:215 is z-30 relative.
findings.fix_sketch: Add class="z-[60]" to both DropdownMenuContent instances (notification dropdown at line 269, user dropdown at line 322) — same fix as BUG-002 applied to DashboardTopbar.svelte:89. Better: fix the primitive once (see UI-003).

findings.title: NotificationBell dropdown z-index not bumped to [60]
findings.evidence: <DropdownMenuContent align="end" class="w-80 p-0"> — no z-[60] override. Inherits the z-50 from primitive dropdown-menu-content.svelte:26. Will be hidden behind the z-50 dialog overlay if a dialog opens while the dropdown is open, and can be clipped by overflow-hidden containers in the header.
findings.fix_sketch: Add z-[60] to the DropdownMenuContent class on line 168. Same primitive-level fix as UI-003 also resolves this.

findings.title: dropdown-menu-content primitive z-50 collides with dialog/drawer/cookie-banner at z-50
findings.evidence: Primitive sets z-50 via cn() in the content class. Conflicting z-50 elements: dialog/dialog-overlay, drawer/drawer-overlay, select-content, tooltip-content, popover-content. The current BUG-002 fix only patches the DashboardTopbar usage; every other consumer (AdminTopbar, NotificationBell, all settings pages, coupons/modifiers/tax/staff dialogs) has the same latent bug.
findings.fix_sketch: Bump the primitive's z-50 to z-[60] (or z-[55]) so it sits above dialogs by default; or define a z-index token (--z-dropdown, --z-dialog, --z-toast) and apply consistently. Then remove the ad-hoc z-[60] overrides from individual callers.

findings.title: Storefront CartDrawer backdrop z-40 collides with CompareBar z-40 (fixed footer bar)
findings.evidence: CartDrawer.svelte:55 backdrop is z-40; CompareBar.svelte:16 (fixed bottom bar) is also z-40. When CartDrawer is open on a page with active comparison items, the CompareBar renders ON TOP of the dark backdrop (z-40 ties → DOM order wins) but UNDER the actual drawer panel (z-50). Visual: the dark overlay doesn't cover the comparison bar, so it appears to 'float'.
findings.fix_sketch: Bump CartDrawer backdrop to z-40 and the drawer panel to z-50 (already done), or hide CompareBar when CartDrawer.open. Cleanest: make CompareBar z-30 (below the backdrop) so the backdrop covers it. Then drawer panel z-50 still pops above.

findings.title: Coupons table edit/delete action buttons are icon-only with no aria-label
findings.evidence: <button onclick={() => openEdit(coupon)} class="p-1.5 rounded hover:bg-muted"><Pencil ... /></button> and the Trash2 sibling. No aria-label, no title. Products page (same project) uses aria-label correctly (line 251/270) — this is inconsistency.
findings.fix_sketch: Add aria-label="Edit coupon" and aria-label="Delete coupon" (or include the coupon code in the label, e.g. aria-label="Edit coupon {coupon.code}") for screen-reader users.

findings.title: Reviews page approve/reject/respond use title= only, not aria-label
findings.evidence: <button onclick={() => approveReview(review.id)} class="..." title="Approve"><Check ... /></button> and the reject + respond siblings. title= provides a hover tooltip but no programmatic name for screen readers.
findings.fix_sketch: Add aria-label="Approve review" / "Reject review" / "Respond to review" (include the review title for context).

findings.title: Brio food theme checkout uses window.alert() for form validation
findings.evidence: if (!name || !phone || (deliveryType === 'delivery' && !address)) { alert('Please fill in all required fields'); return; }. Native alert() breaks the custom-themed UX, is not stylable, and is jarring vs. the rest of the Brio theme's polished components.
findings.fix_sketch: Replace alert() with inline error messages under each field (red text, field-level highlight) plus a toast at the top. Use the same theme tokens (t.primaryColor, t.textColor) for consistency. Mark required fields with a visible asterisk.

findings.title: Coupon/Support/Shipping dialogs use toast.error only — no inline field-level errors
findings.evidence: handleSave()/handleSubmit() check for required fields and return toast.error('Code and discount value required') etc. Users don't see WHICH field is wrong without scanning the whole form. Inconsistent with the storefront's superForm-based pages (e.g. storefront/(auth)/login/+page.svelte:69-86) which DO show inline errors.
findings.fix_sketch:
Add per-field error state in form object (e.g. formErrors.code = 'Required'), render

{formErrors.code}

under each input, and only fall back to toast.error for network/server errors.

findings.title: Dashboard app.css body hardcodes hex colors instead of using --color-background/--color-foreground tokens
findings.evidence: body { font-family: var(--font-body); background: #060b14; color: #e8edf5; } — but the @theme inline block on lines 8-9 defines --color-background: #060b14; and --color-foreground: #e8edf5; exactly. mode-watcher (sonner.svelte:14) is wired up but the body styles will not adapt if a future light mode is introduced.
findings.fix_sketch: Change to background: var(--color-background); color: var(--color-foreground); so dark/light theming works without further edits. Same for any inline `style="background: #060b14"` in components.

findings.title: Products table Name column does not truncate long product titles
findings.evidence: {product.titleEn}...{product.titleAr} — no truncate, no max-w-*, no whitespace-nowrap. A 200-char product name will wrap and break the row height, especially on smaller screens. Compare the customers/orders pages that use truncate max-w-* on name columns.
findings.fix_sketch: Wrap the titleEn + titleAr spans in a min-w-0 div with truncate / max-w-[300px], and use line-clamp-2 if you want to show the full name in two lines. Apply the same fix to other long-text cells in Orders (email + phone) and Customers (tag lists).
